assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md table shows 2.1.0 and 2.1.1 marked 'YES' with h2 0.4.5, and all 2.2.x versions (2.2.0-2.2.4) marked 'NO' with h2 >= 0.4.8. Notes reference specific source tags and lock file data (e.g., 'Source tag v0.3.8', 'Source tag v0.4.5').

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md proposes versions [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1] only. RHTPA 2.2.0 is explicitly marked 'REMOVE — h2 0.4.8 is at or above the fix threshold. Not affected.' The Jira mutation shows only 2.1.x versions in the versions array.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 8 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md states 'Remediation tasks are created for stream 2.1.x only. No remediation needed for stream 2.2.x.' Both tasks target release/0.3.z (2.1.x branch). Task 1 summary: 'Remediate CVE-2026-33501: bump h2 to 0.4.8 (2.1.x)'. Task 2 targets rhtpa-release.0.3.z. Post-triage summary confirms 'Stream 2.2.x: no remediation needed — all versions ship h2 >= 0.4.8.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md stream scope resolution states: 'The issue summary contains no stream suffix. This issue is unscoped — it covers all configured version streams (2.1.x and 2.2.x). Steps 2-8 will analyze all streams.' version-impact.md table includes all 7 versions across both streams (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4).

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 8 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md explicitly states: 'No cross-stream impact notice is required because this issue is unscoped (covers all streams). There are no "other streams outside the issue's scope" since the issue has no scope restriction.' Notes section reiterates: 'Case B does not apply to unscoped issues since there are no "other streams outside the issue's scope."'
